title: Brevo vs Buttondown (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare Brevo and Buttondown email marketing tools. See pricing, features, and which suits your needs best.
h1: Brevo vs Buttondown: Detailed Comparison for 2025
intro: Brevo and Buttondown are both freemium email marketing platforms, but they serve very different audiences. Brevo is an all-in-one CRM suite built for businesses needing multi-channel marketing and transactional email, while Buttondown specializes in newsletter creation for developers and technical writers. Your choice depends on whether you need a comprehensive marketing platform or a focused, markdown-friendly newsletter tool.
choose_a_if: You need multi-channel marketing (email, SMS, WhatsApp, live chat) in one platform
You require transactional email API for application notifications
You're running an ecommerce business or larger operation needing CRM features
You want to scale to 100,000+ free contacts before upgrading
choose_b_if: You prefer writing newsletters in markdown
You're a developer or technical writer looking for simplicity
You want to offer paid subscriptions to your newsletter readers
You need affordable pricing with API access for automation

faq.a: It depends on your needs. Brevo is better if you need a comprehensive CRM with multi-channel marketing capabilities. Buttondown is better if you're a developer or writer wanting a simple, markdown-focused newsletter platform. Both excel in their respective niches.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, Brevo or Buttondown?
faq.a: Buttondown's paid plan starts at $9/month compared to Brevo's $25/month. However, Brevo's free tier supports 100,000 subscribers versus Buttondown's 100, making Brevo better value if you have many free contacts.

faq.q: Can I switch from Buttondown to Brevo?
faq.a: Yes, both platforms offer import/export functionality. Buttondown explicitly lists import/export as a feature, and Brevo's broader feature set makes it easy to migrate your subscriber list and campaigns. Check your current provider's documentation for specific migration steps.
